mission: Validate webcam-based eye tracking as $0 alternative to $500 Pupil Labs hardware for Boly 2017 bistable protocol

boly_2017_protocol.citation: Frassle et al. (Boly group context) — Combined fMRI- and eye movement-based decoding of bistable plaid motion perception. NeuroImage 2017/2018, doi:10.1016/j.neuroimage.2017.12.073, PMID 29294388
boly_2017_protocol.method: Bistable plaid motion + OKN (optokinetic nystagmus) decoding via SVM. Eye-only classifier achieved 88% accuracy; combined with fMRI -> 91%.
boly_2017_protocol.core_signal: OKN slow-phase direction reversal at perceptual switch (no overt report).
boly_2017_protocol.min_sample_rate_rationale: OKN slow-phase velocity ~5-30 deg/s; quick-phase saccades ~30-50 ms; per Nyquist for ~3 Hz OKN cycle frequency, >=30 Hz is the practical minimum for direction estimation. Original Boly study used research-grade trackers at 250-1000 Hz.
boly_2017_protocol.min_spatial_accuracy_rationale: OKN direction (left-vs-right slow phase) needs sub-degree velocity vector resolution; coarse fixation suffices for gaze position but velocity requires fine inter-frame displacement <=0.5 deg precision.

candidates.name: WebGazer.js
candidates.spatial_accuracy_source: webgazer.cs.brown.edu (~4 deg); Steffan et al. 2024 Infancy / PMC10841511 reports 3.94 deg in-lab fixation offset (172 px / 15% screen), 18% (~4.7 deg) online
candidates.sample_rate_source: PCIbex/visual-world replication studies; Yang & Krajbich 2021; harvardlds.org child-language assessment
candidates.anima_compat: browser HTML page + LSL marker bridge to OpenBCI
candidates.notes: Self-calibrates from click-stream interactions; no infrared. CPU-heavy (~19% Chrome single-core).

candidates.name: GazeRecorder / GazeFlow / GazeCloudAPI
candidates.license: FREE for non-commercial; commercial license required for paid use
candidates.spatial_accuracy_source: gazerecorder.com self-report 0.9-1.0 deg vs SMI RED-250; independent benchmarks (Springer RPA pilot 2025) confirm best-in-class among webcam tools
candidates.temporal_sync_precision_ms: ~30-60 (cloud-API roundtrip adds latency vs local SDK)
candidates.anima_compat: GazeFlow SDK local (Windows-only); GazeCloudAPI browser-embeddable, lower CPU (~3%) than WebGazer
candidates.notes: Closed-source vendor binary. Self-reported accuracy not peer-reviewed. License terms restrict research-publication redistribution.

deferred_pyGaze.reason: pyGaze is a wrapper around hardware tracker drivers (EyeLink, Tobii, SMI, Pupil) — NOT an inference engine. Without a backing tracker it provides no gaze signal. Out of scope for $0 webcam-only fallback.
